policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released binding guidelines requiring all SCBs to integrate climate risk into ICAAP and stress testing by March 2027, with mandatory quarterly reporting of physical risk exposure by sector. Banks with assets over ₹10 lakh crore must establish dedicated climate risk committees at board level.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies with Assurance Mandate
policy.description: SEBI extended mandatory BRSR Core reporting with reasonable assurance to top 500 companies by market cap from FY2026-27, adding Scope 3 emissions disclosure for high-impact sectors. Value chain ESG assessments now required for top 250 companies.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Sandbox Framework for Index-Based Climate Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI published regulatory sandbox guidelines specifically for parametric climate insurance, allowing 24-month pilot periods with relaxed solvency requirements for products covering flood, cyclone, and heat stress triggers. Framework permits API-based claims settlement under 72 hours.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Climate Risk Pillar 3 Disclosure Framework
policy.description: BCBS issued final Pillar 3 disclosure requirements for climate-related financial risks, mandating standardized templates for transition and physical risk exposures effective January 2028. Includes specific metrics for real estate and agriculture portfolios.

policy.title: ISSB Publishes Guidance on Proportionate Application for Emerging Market Jurisdictions
policy.description: IFRS Foundation released implementation guidance allowing phased ISSB adoption for emerging markets, with simplified Scope 3 calculation methodologies for companies lacking supply chain data. Transition relief extends to 2029 for SMEs in developing economies.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: Network for Greening the Financial System published updated scenarios incorporating IPCC AR6 data with sub-national physical risk projections for 180 countries. New acute hazard modules cover compound events and include India-specific monsoon variability pathways.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Analytics in India
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a multi-year partnership with HDFC Bank to provide ClimateScore Financial analytics for commercial real estate and infrastructure lending portfolios across India. Deal reportedly valued at $4-5M annually with exclusive arrangement through 2029.

competitors.title: Sprih Closes $18M Series B to Expand Climate Risk Modules for Indian Banks
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih raised Series B led by Accel and Bertelsmann India, with participation from existing investor Sequoia Surge. Funds earmarked for new physical risk assessment product targeting NBFC and mid-market bank compliance with RBI guidelines.

competitors.title: MSCI Launches India Physical Climate Risk Ratings with District-Level Granularity
competitors.description: MSCI ESG Research introduced physical risk ratings for 3,200+ Indian corporate locations mapped to 750+ districts, covering flood, cyclone, water stress, and extreme heat. Product integrates directly with existing MSCI ESG Manager platform for FI clients.

competitors.title: XDI Expands APAC Coverage with New Asset-Level Database for South Asia
competitors.description: Cross Dependency Initiative launched physical risk analytics covering 12 million geolocated assets across India, Bangladesh, and Sri Lanka. Partnership with Swiss Re announced for embedding XDI scores in reinsurance pricing for South Asian markets.

competitors.title: Moody's Integrates Physical Risk Scores into Credit Rating Methodologies for Indian Corporates
competitors.description: Moody's announced that climate physical risk scores will become explicit factors in rating methodologies for Indian infrastructure and real estate issuers from Q3 2026. Integration covers 400+ rated Indian entities with material physical exposure.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Signs with Federal Bank for Agri-Climate Risk Assessment Platform
competitors.description: Kochi-based CarbonMint partnered with Federal Bank to deploy climate risk analytics for agricultural loan portfolios, covering 1.2 million farmer accounts. Platform combines satellite imagery with parametric trigger monitoring for crop insurance integration.

research.title: Climate Physical Risk and Bank Lending: Evidence from Emerging Markets
research.description: Study of 340 banks across 45 emerging markets found 18% average increase in NPL ratios for portfolios concentrated in high physical risk geographies. Indian banks showed particular vulnerability in coastal infrastructure and rainfed agriculture lending.

research.title: Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Scaling Climate Resilience for Financial Inclusion
research.description: IFC analysis projects $2.8 billion addressable market for parametric climate products in India by 2030, with flood and cyclone triggers showing highest demand. Report identifies bank distribution channels as key enabler, citing 4x penetration potential versus standalone insurers.

research.title: sigma 2/2026: Natural Catastrophes in 2025 — A Year of Compounding Perils
research.description: Global insured nat-cat losses reached $158 billion in 2025, with India recording $8.2 billion in economic losses from monsoon floods and Cyclone Anika. Report highlights 35% protection gap growth in emerging Asia since 2020.

research.title: ESG Data Availability in Emerging Markets: Closing the Information Gap
research.description: UNEP FI assessment found only 23% of Indian listed companies provide decision-useful physical risk disclosures despite BRSR requirements. Data quality scores lowest for Scope 3 emissions and supply chain risk in agriculture, textiles, and chemicals sectors.

research.title: Integrating Climate Risk into Supervisory Practices: Lessons from Early Movers
research.description: BIS survey of 28 central banks found most lacking standardized methodologies for climate-adjusted PD/LGD estimation. Paper recommends hazard-specific calibration using granular geospatial data rather than sector-level proxies currently dominant.

research.title: Climate Risk Assessment for Indian Agriculture: District-Level Projections to 2050
research.description: CRISIL analysis identifies 127 districts across Maharashtra, MP, Karnataka, and Andhra Pradesh at severe risk of yield decline exceeding 25% by 2050. Report maps exposure to ₹4.2 lakh crore in agricultural credit outstanding.

ourRead.headline: RBI's binding climate risk guidelines create an 18-month compliance runway where StepChange's PD/LGD models become essential infrastructure for Indian banks.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: climate risk quantification for Indian financial institutions is transitioning from voluntary exploration to regulatory mandate. The combined force of RBI's final guidelines, SEBI's expanded BRSR requirements, and IRDAI's parametric sandbox creates a synchronized regulatory push that will drive urgent demand across all three of StepChange's strategic bets.

The RBI guidelines (policy-1) are the most consequential development. The March 2027 deadline for integrating climate risk into ICAAP means banks have roughly 11 months to operationalize physical risk analytics — far shorter than typical procurement cycles. The quarterly sectoral reporting requirement specifically demands the kind of granular, portfolio-level risk data that StepChange's hazard-based PD/LGD models provide. Critically, the BIS research (research-5) validates our approach: regulators are explicitly rejecting sector-level proxies in favor of geospatial hazard-specific calibration. StepChange should position our methodology as regulatory-ready and compliant with emerging Basel standards.

The competitor landscape is heating up rapidly. Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) and MSCI's India-specific product launch (comp-3) signal that global players are prioritizing the Indian market. However, both are bringing solutions designed for developed market contexts. The World Bank study (research-1) showing Indian banks' particular vulnerability in coastal infrastructure and rainfed agriculture points to where StepChange's local hazard data and sectoral expertise should create differentiation. Sprih's Series B for climate risk modules (comp-2) is the most direct competitive threat — we should accelerate enterprise sales conversations before their new product reaches market.

The IFC parametric insurance report (research-2) provides powerful ammunition for our second strategic bet. The $2.8 billion market projection and the finding that bank distribution channels offer 4x penetration potential directly validates StepChange's positioning. Combined with IRDAI's new sandbox framework (policy-3), we have regulatory cover and market sizing to accelerate conversations with bank partners on parametric product design. The CRISIL district-level analysis (research-6) mapping ₹4.2 lakh crore in exposed agricultural credit gives us specific geographies and portfolios to target.

Watch closely: Jupiter's HDFC deal terms and whether they pursue other large private banks. Action item: Fast-track a joint proposal with an IRDAI-registered insurer to enter the parametric sandbox before Sprih's new product launches — first-mover advantage in the sandbox will translate to regulatory relationships that matter when guidelines become binding.
